predictions.description: XPath string read of A-1 amount preserves the trailing-zero decimal exactly ("1234.5600", not 1234.56)
XPath string read of A-2 big int (>2^53, odd — would collapse to ...994 under float) is string-exact
namespaced aud:note read via local-name() decodes & exactly once

OUT-OF-BAND: harness reads the saved file directly — full serialization pinned: XML declaration kept, version attribute updated in place to 2, xmlns:aud declaration + aud:note prefix intact, status now 'posted', entities single-encoded, empty <customer></customer> re-serialized self-closed by xmldom, decimals/big ints/dates byte-identical to the fixture

equals: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?><invoices xmlns:aud="urn:sgt:audit" version="2"><invoice id="A-1"><customer>Müller & Söhne 漢字 🎯</customer><amount>1234.5600</amount><big>9007199254740993</big><status>posted</status><posted>2028-02-29</posted><aud:note>seed & keep</aud:note></invoice><invoice id="A-2"><customer/><amount>-0.10</amount><big>9007199254740995</big><status>hold</status><posted>2026-12-31T23:59:59</posted></invoice></invoices>

predictions.description: the save cycle never double-encodes entities (&amp; absent from the saved bytes)
xml-to-json decodes the entity once and preserves unicode (umlaut, CJK, emoji) in the customer value
fast-xml-parser leaves the >2^53 integer as a STRING (strnum precision guard) — no silent ...995 → ...996 collapse at the XML→JSON seam

KNOWN-COERCION (TODOS.md 'xml-to-json numeric tag coercion'): xml-to-json hardcodes fast-xml-parser numeric tag parsing — '1234.5600' becomes the NUMBER 1234.56 and the trailing-zero scale is silently lost at this seam. This prediction pins today's behavior as a characterization; Byron must rule whether the action grows a preserve-strings knob (the exactness contract suggests it should).

json-to-csv output is byte-pinned: column union in first-seen order (attributes after elements), number-coerced amount renders 1234.56/-0.1, big ints string-exact, blank customer and missing aud:note both render as empty fields (the CSV seam cannot distinguish blank from missing — pinned)
the draft→posted mutation made in the XML document is visible in the final DataTable cell (whole-chain witness)
the >2^53 big int arrives in the final DataTable string-exact after XML→file→JSON→CSV→DataTable
